brief_summary: Two-column ledger. Left (coral solid borders, 'what they're buying'): power profile (Zephyr-native BLE, 660-hour claim) and Bluetooth maturity (5 years of DIY field testing). Right (bordeaux dashed borders, 'what they're inheriting'): configurator gap (ZMK Studio vs VIA, devicetree leakage) and advanced macros/combos gap (docs gap, not feature gap). Footer: 'a vendor taking a firmware position inherits the firmware's reputation'.
data_sources: zmk-mainstream-shift.mdx § 'The strengths the vendor side is buying' — 'The first is the power profile ... Keychron's 660-hour claim is the flag the lineup is planted on ... The second is Bluetooth maturity. The wireless DIY cohort spent five years shaking the protocol-level bugs out of ZMK's BLE stack on real hardware'

zmk-mainstream-shift.mdx § 'The weaknesses they're inheriting' — 'The first is the configurator. QMK has VIA ... ZMK's answer is ZMK Studio, which is in active development and showing real progress, but is not yet the drop-in equivalent ... The second is feature parity on advanced macros and combos. QMK's combo and macro infrastructure has accumulated a decade of community-driven features — Tap Dance, Layer Lock, Mod-Tap nuance'
